_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning ways to work safely and look after ourselves when we are doing things on our own.

_source.w: We are learning ways to work safely and look after ourselves when we are doing things on our own.
y: Being able to work independently means we can get things done even when a teacher isn't right beside us.
Knowing how to stay safe helps us take care of ourselves and the people around us.
Working safely builds trust so we can take on more responsibility as we grow.
eg: We might practise putting away scissors carefully after we finish cutting.
We could follow classroom rules for moving around safely, like walking instead of running inside.
We might learn how to pack up our own workspace neatly after finishing an activity.
We could practise following a set of instructions step by step without needing a reminder.
We might talk about what to do if something feels unsafe, like telling a teacher straight away.
We could learn to use classroom tools like glue and rulers properly so nobody gets hurt.
